things.name: Senso-ji Temple
things.description: Tokyo's oldest temple in Asakusa with the iconic Kaminarimon Thunder Gate

things.name: Shibuya Crossing
things.description: World's busiest pedestrian crossing with up to 3,000 people crossing at once

things.name: Tokyo Skytree
things.description: World's tallest broadcasting tower at 634m with observation decks at 350m and 450m

things.name: Meiji Shrine
things.description: Serene forested Shinto shrine in the heart of Harajuku dedicated to Emperor Meiji

things.name: Shinjuku Gyoen
things.description: Former imperial garden combining Japanese, French and English garden styles

things.name: TeamLab Borderless
things.description: Immersive digital art museum where the artworks move through the entire building

things.name: Akihabara Electric Town
things.description: Tokyo's tech and anime district with multi-storey electronics and manga shops

things.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
things.description: Fish market atmosphere with the world's finest sushi for breakfast

things.name: Imperial Palace East Gardens
things.description: Former inner citadel of Edo Castle with traditional Japanese landscape garden

things.name: Harajuku & Takeshita Street
things.description: Youth fashion capital and birthplace of Lolita and kawaii street style

things.name: Tower of London
things.description: Historic castle and former royal palace on the north bank of the Thames

things.name: Buckingham Palace
things.description: Official London residence and administrative headquarters of the British monarch

things.name: British Museum
things.description: World-class collection of human history, art and culture spanning two million years

things.name: Tower Bridge
things.description: Iconic Victorian bascule bridge and London landmark opening over the River Thames

things.name: Westminster Abbey
things.description: Gothic abbey church and site of British royal coronations since 1066

things.name: National Gallery
things.description: Art museum on Trafalgar Square housing over 2,300 paintings from 1250 to 1900

things.name: Hyde Park
things.description: 350-acre royal park in central London famous for the Serpentine and Speaker's Corner

things.name: St Paul's Cathedral
things.description: Sir Christopher Wren's Baroque masterpiece with its iconic dome dominating the City skyline

things.name: Tate Modern
things.description: Leading modern art gallery in a converted Bankside power station

things.name: Trafalgar Square
things.description: Public plaza centred on Nelson's Column, heart of the capital's civic life
